title: Unilateral nasal obstruction documentation

red_flags.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.
red_flags.rationale: The exact rhinitis history section supports documenting unilateral obstruction, duration, discharge, bleeding, smell, triggers, sleep and impact without assigning rhinitis or structural disease.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors

examination.text: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed nasal, oral and upper-airway assessment without auto-populating findings.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact safety-net section supports documenting clinician-entered plans and review only when completed.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

referral.text: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.
referral.rationale: The exact head-and-neck section supports clinician concern and reviewed specialist information in qualified contexts without asserting cancer.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8.1–1.8.5 — laryngeal, oral, and thyroid cancer recognition features

sources_used.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation

source_limitations: Paediatric foreign body, trauma, exact nasal findings, diagnosis, investigation, and management remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
